entries.value: Target occupancy rate is 65-80% depending on market and property type. Urban properties in high-demand markets should target 75-85%. Rural/seasonal properties may see 50-65% annually but should hit 90%+ in peak season. Occupancy above 90% year-round likely means your pricing is too low.

Average Daily Rate (ADR) is the average nightly revenue per booked night. ADR = total accommodation revenue / number of booked nights. Track separately by property, channel, and season. ADR should increase 3-8% year-over-year in a healthy market. Falling ADR with stable occupancy signals under-pricing.

Revenue Per Available Night (RevPAN) = ADR x Occupancy Rate. This is the single most important metric for STR portfolio health because it balances rate and occupancy. A $200 ADR at 60% occupancy ($120 RevPAN) outperforms $150 ADR at 75% occupancy ($112.50 RevPAN). Optimize RevPAN, not occupancy or ADR alone.

Target overall guest satisfaction of 4.7+/5.0 across all platforms. Airbnb Superhost requires 4.8+ overall rating. VRBO Premier Host requires 4.5+. Below 4.5 triggers search ranking penalties on most platforms. Track category scores separately: cleanliness (target 4.8+), communication (4.9+), check-in (4.8+), accuracy (4.7+), location (4.6+), value (4.5+).

Target response time under 15 minutes for all guest inquiries. Airbnb Superhost requires 90% response rate within 24 hours, but competitive hosts respond within 1 hour. First response under 5 minutes increases booking conversion by 40%. Set up auto-responses for overnight messages (11pm-7am) to maintain metrics.

Target 100% review response rate. Respond to every review within 48 hours. Positive reviews get a personalized thank-you (mention specifics they enjoyed). Negative reviews get a professional, empathetic response acknowledging the issue and explaining corrective action. Never argue. Platform algorithms boost listings with high host response rates.

Target cleaning turnaround under 4 hours from guest check-out to next guest readiness. For same-day turnovers (check-out 11am, check-in 3pm), the window is typically 4 hours. Build in 30-minute inspection buffer. Late check-outs or early check-ins compress the window — charge fees or block the option if turnaround is consistently tight.

Target cancellation rate below 5% of confirmed bookings. Host-initiated cancellations are severely penalized (Airbnb: search ranking drop, $50-$100 fee, potential Superhost loss). Guest cancellations above 10% indicate pricing/market mismatch. Use strict cancellation policies for peak dates and moderate policies for off-peak to balance flexibility and revenue certainty.

Target repeat guest rate above 15%. Repeat guests have zero acquisition cost, tend to book direct (avoiding 15% channel fees), leave better reviews, and cause less property wear. Track repeat rate by property and overall. Incentivize with 10% returning guest discount on direct bookings. Collect email at check-in for direct marketing.

No single channel should represent more than 60% of bookings. Diversification protects against channel policy changes, fee increases, or account suspension. Target mix: Airbnb 35-45%, VRBO 20-30%, Direct 15-25%, Other (Lodgify, Booking.com) 10-15%. Direct bookings are highest margin — invest in a direct booking website.

Airbnb Superhost criteria (assessed quarterly): 10+ completed stays OR 3+ stays totaling 100+ nights, 90%+ response rate, <1% host-initiated cancellation rate, 4.8+ overall rating. VRBO Premier Host: 4.5+ rating, <3% cancellation, 90% response within 24h, 10+ completed bookings. Losing Superhost status reduces bookings 15-30%.

High season pricing: 1.3-1.8x base rate (beach summer, ski winter, holiday weekends). Shoulder season: 1.0-1.2x base rate. Low season: 0.7-0.9x base rate. Major events/holidays: up to 2.5x base rate if demand supports it. Weekend premium: 1.15-1.35x weekday rate. Never price below your cost floor (cleaning + supplies + mortgage/rent portion).

Minimum stay strategy: Weekends (Fri-Sun) 2-night minimum, Peak season 3-7 night minimum, Holiday weeks 5-7 night minimum, Off-peak weekdays 1-night okay. Longer minimum stays reduce turnover cost and increase RevPAN. One-night stays are rarely profitable after cleaning costs. Gap nights (orphan days between bookings) destroy revenue — use pricing tools to fill them.

If a date is unbooked within 48 hours, apply a 15-25% last-minute discount. Revenue from a discounted night always beats $0 from an empty night. Automate: 72 hours out apply 10% discount, 48 hours out apply 20%, 24 hours out apply 25%. Never discount more than 30% — below that signals desperation and attracts problem guests.

Dynamic pricing adjustments should never exceed 2.0x base rate (ceiling) or drop below 0.5x base rate (floor). Set absolute minimum nightly rate = (monthly fixed costs / 30) + cleaning cost amortized + $20 buffer. Review pricing tool suggestions weekly — auto-pricing tools sometimes race to the bottom in low-demand periods.

Send 5 automated messages per booking: (1) Booking confirmation with excitement and house rules, (2) Pre-arrival 3 days out with check-in instructions and WiFi password, (3) Check-in day morning with lockbox code and local recommendations, (4) Mid-stay check-in at day 2 asking if everything is satisfactory, (5) Check-out morning with departure instructions and review request. Personalize each with guest name and property details.

Use a channel manager (Lodgify, Hostaway, Guesty) to sync calendars and prevent double bookings. Sync calendars every 15 minutes minimum. When a booking comes in on one channel, all other channels must block those dates within 5 minutes. Manual calendar management across 3+ channels is a double-booking waiting to happen. Always maintain one source-of-truth calendar.

ADR (Average Daily Rate) = total accommodation revenue divided by number of booked nights. Excludes cleaning fees and service fees. Used alongside occupancy rate and RevPAN as the three core STR revenue metrics. Not to be confused with 'nightly rate' which is the listed price — ADR is the realized average after discounts, promotions, and length-of-stay adjustments.

A gap night (orphan night) is an unbooked night sandwiched between two bookings that is too short for a new reservation (typically 1-2 nights when minimum stay is 3). Gap nights are lost revenue. Prevention strategies: adjust minimum stay rules around existing bookings, offer discounts to extend adjacent stays, or temporarily lower minimum stay for gap dates.
